answer:
The original train length is \(12 \times 15 = 180\) meters, and it crosses a post in 18 seconds, so its speed is \(180/18 = 10\) m/s. After detaching two bogies, the length becomes \(10 \times 15 = 150\) meters. At the same speed, the time to cross the post is \(150/10 = 15\) seconds.

\boxed{C}
